content.problemStatement: Write a program using a for loop to find and print the index of the first occurrence of the number 8 in the array **{2, 4, 8, 12, 8}**.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 100), the number of test cases. No further data is required for each test case.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single integer — the 0‑based index of the first occurrence of the number 8 in the fixed array {2,4,8,12,8}. Print each answer on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is a single test case. The fixed array's first 8 is at index 2, so the program prints 2.
Three test cases are processed independently; each yields the same answer 2.
Five test cases – the result does not depend on any input data, only on the constant array.

hidden.label: general case 1 – medium number of cases

solution.approach: The array is fixed and known: {2,4,8,12,8}. Scanning it once reveals that the first 8 appears at index 2. Because the answer does not depend on any input data, we can simply output 2 for every test case. The algorithm reads T and prints "2" T times.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time operation per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used regardless of T.
